justification: O(n) dual-Set algorithm used (not Map, but still O(n) — satisfies rubric). AC4 correctly returns each duplicate exactly once via Set guarantees. AC4 edge case (3+ occurrences) explicitly tested. Notes explain why O(n) was chosen and that O(n²) would take >10,000ms. Confidence section notes AC3 timing sensitivity on slow hardware (runtime validation concern). All 5 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage.

observations.text: Dual-Set approach is elegant — avoids Map counting overhead while maintaining O(n) guarantee
AC4 edge case explicitly tested with IDs appearing 3+ times
All 5 acceptance criteria mapped with status, test name, and notes
Deviation documented for AC3 test threshold (200ms vs 100ms) with clear justification
Confidence section acknowledges AC3 timing flakiness on slow CI — addresses runtime validation concern
Confidence section could more explicitly state that O(n) complexity is a design property not fully verifiable by unit test, rather than framing it only as CI timing flakiness
